name: The Gadarene demoniac
notes: A threefold synoptic retelling of Jesus crossing to the other side and casting the devils out of the man (or men) among the tombs. The shared core runs detail by detail: the coming to the country of the Gadarenes/Gergesenes, the tomb-dwelling possessed meeting him, the cry "What have I to do with thee, Jesus, thou Son of God ... torment," the devils' plea to enter the swine, the herd running violently down a steep place into the sea and perishing, the keepers fleeing to tell it in the city, and the people beseeching Jesus to depart out of their coasts. Mark and Luke alone name the devils "Legion, for we are many," show the man afterward clothed and in his right mind, and record him sent home to publish what was done; these details are linked between those two. Matthew names two possessed while Mark and Luke fix on one, and Matthew writes "Gergesenes" where Mark and Luke write "Gadarenes"; these are complementary vantages on one account, left as matters for further study.
